subject: Can you build a road from one of your non-blocked roads if other player's settlement is at the other endpoint of the constructed road?

posts.body:
'-' - my existing roads, 'X' - other player's settlement, '=' - road I want to build; is the following setup legal, i.e. can I build the '=' road:

      = X
   / 
  |

?
Really can't figure it out from your "graphics". Could you take a photo and upload it instead?
I agree that a photo would be infinitely more clear. But I'm pretty sure what you want is not allowed.

Do you mean "can I continue to build on a road where another player placed a settlement between two road pieces of mine", then the answer is yes if the intersection you want to build past has no other player's piece (settlement, city, C&K knight) on it. Also, you can extend your road network to other players' settlements to add another piece to your bid for longest road.

Can I build a road on an edge where the intersection connecting to my road has another player's piece? Then the answer is no.

vshorb wrote:

'-' - my existing roads, 'X' - other player's settlement, '=' - road I want to build; is the following setup legal, i.e. can I build the '=' road:

Forget hexagons...straight line for example:

- - - = X
Yes, you can build.

- - - X =
No, you cannot build.

- - X - =
Yes, you can build.

To put it into words: 

If a road ends in an empty intersection you can expand from that end. Whether the newly built road ends at an opponent's village or city afterwards does not matter.

Got it, thanks! Yes, the rules seem pretty clear about disallowing building roads from the intersection if there's an opponent's village/city there. My question was if I can build a road starting from a "valid" intersection if it will end at the opponent's village/city - this seems like a useful way to block opponent from expanding in this direction. Seems like it's allowed, thanks everyone for your answers
